issues: The Seraph pages now read more like manuscript instruments, but they are still materially short of the reference images in overall composition and decorative density. The large-shape silhouette, border choreography, and side-rail drama are still more generic and boxy than the references.

The palette is closer but still not exact enough. The current brass, teal, and parchment relationships remain somewhat flat and evenly distributed rather than matching the sharper focal accents and aged-paper tonal hierarchy implied by the references.

The newly added reusable primitives help architecture, but the page-level compositions still rely heavily on CSS-painted ornament rather than a fuller set of reusable section-level Seraph components for manuscript plates, illuminated margins, emblem stacks, and page dividers.

The bestiary page still feels more like a list of task cards than a reconstructed illuminated folio. It needs more asymmetry, more illustrated-manuscript framing, and stronger integration between the creature plate and the surrounding decorative system.

Storybook primitive coverage improved, but the page-specific fidelity controls are still shallow relative to the target. There is still not enough component-level configurability around border variants, ornament density, side-rail motifs, and page section arrangements.
Code and diff surfaces are being reused, which is good, but they still read as embedded modern controls rather than fully assimilated Seraph manuscript instruments.
strengths: The implementation moved real shared source, not superficial wrappers.
Seraph primitive reusability improved through configurable eyebrow, footer, tone, folio-border variant, rail caption, and margin-note support.
The button primary state contrast issue is improved by explicit foreground and darker active states.
The diff tab states now have better dark-mode treatment and clearer active emphasis.
a11yRisks: The decorative Seraph surfaces still rely on several muted brass-on-paper and teal-on-paper combinations that likely remain borderline in small text contexts.
Blueprint-tone task cards may still need a more systematic contrast audit for body copy, metadata, and ornament overlays.

Broader dark-mode consistency is still incomplete because this pass improved shared controls and some mock surfaces, but did not complete a full state-by-state audit across all affected examples and stories.
nextEdits: Push the Seraph page skeletons closer to the references by redesigning the macro layout: stronger outer frame geometry, more distinct manuscript side columns, and a less rectangular card rhythm.

Extract additional reusable Seraph sections instead of encoding more layout identity in the page bodies: illuminated section plates, margin gloss panels, emblem/totem stacks, ornamental dividers, and hero manuscript slabs.
Rework the bestiary composition specifically so the illustration plate and surrounding annotations feel like one artifact rather than a task-list plus image slot.
Tune the Seraph palette more aggressively against the references, especially the parchment depth, brass highlight range, and teal/cinnabar focal distribution.
Assimilate code and diff surfaces further into the Seraph language with stronger framing, labels, and ornamental casing while preserving their shared reusability.
Continue the a11y pass on dark and interactive states, especially small metadata, hover states, disabled controls, and blueprint-toned surfaces.
